commits.messageBody:
The base Harness.updateState path (used by task tools like task_write,
task_complete) writes to the base state via applyStateUpdates but does
not go through HarnessCompat.setState, so the V1 session never receives
the update.  HarnessCompat.getState() merges session state over base
state, causing the session's stale tasks:[] default to shadow the real
task list.

Fix:
- Make Harness.applyStateUpdates protected so subclasses can intercept
  state writes from both setState and updateState paths.
- Override applyStateUpdates in HarnessCompat to forward non-session
  fields to the V1 session, keeping both stores in sync.

Co-Authored-By: tyler <tylerdbarnes@gmail.com>
commits.messageHeadline: fix: sync task state to V1 session in HarnessCompat

title: fix: sync task state to V1 session in HarnessCompat
